title: Technology Stack | Logos
description: Modular by design. Storage, Messaging, Blockchain, and User Modules form the foundation for privacy-first decentralised applications.
heading: The Logos Technology Stack

sections.eyebrow: Open by design, open in spirit. Build truly decentralised applications with the modular Logos Technology Stack.
sections.headline: The Logos Technology Stack
sections.body: The Logos Technology Stack is a unified ecosystem of distinct modules. The entire stack is modular so developers can assemble their own Logos-based platform with a different selection of modules.

status.label: Current Status
status.body: Testnet v0.1 launched in March 2026. This is the first release where all modules are packaged tog...

sections.title: The Stack
sections.eyebrow: Disclaimer: Abstract representation of the stack.

architecture.title: Architecture Overview
body: Logos provides developers with a modular, plugin-based runtime for building decentralised applications.

This architecture allows nodes to dynamically discover and load modules while preserving privacy. This approach allows the network to evolve and provides a foundation for decentralised applications and social institutions that remain resistant to capture and corruption – whether deployed by local communities or nation‑state operators.

pillars.title: Storage
pillars.body: Decentralised file storage and retrieval, using content-addressed (CID-based) data

pillars.title: User Modules
pillars.body: Anyone can build modules that plug into the same IPC infrastructure.

sections.networkingTitle:
The Networking Stack:
Discovery, Peering, and Mix-Net
sections.networkingDescription: This layer handles how Logos nodes find each other, establish connections, and communicate.
sections.foundationDescription: A microkernel that handles the essential primitives every decentralised application needs.

sections.title: Logos Basecamp
sections.description: Basecamp is a a complete distribution that bundles the kernel, the default modules, and UI packages into a usable product. It provides the primary user interface, hosting “Simple Apps” that let users interact with the various modules

body: Most blockchain platforms provide consensus only — you bring your own messaging and storage, or rely on centralised fallbacks. Logos ships all three as a unified stack with privacy at every layer.

Bitcoin proved that decentralised value transactions are possible. Ethereum generalised this for arbitrary computation. Logos extends it further: a platform for deploying arbitrary social institutions — governance systems, communication networks, data archives — with network-level and transaction-level privacy.

Where Zcash focuses on private transactions, Logos provides a full application platform. Where Polkadot focuses on chain interoperability, Logos focuses on privacy and self-governance. Where Cosmos provides a framework for sovereign chains, Logos provides a complete integrated stack.

sections.title: Open Source
sections.subtitle: All Logos code is open source, licensed under permissive licences. The primary development happens across several GitHub organisations.

rows.title: logos-co
rows.description: 132 repositories: Basecamp, module system, protocol modules, LEZ, SDKs, dev tooling

sections.heading: Use Cases
sections.subheading: We know today’s systems are captured and brittle. We build alternatives to give people a choice. Build on Logos and serve your community.
sections.subheadingExtra: Privacy-preserving blockchain for sovereign order and decentralised governance.
